summary: Gruber examines the design tradeoff behind the iPhone's mute switch behavior after an iPhone alarm disrupted a New York Philharmonic performance of Mahler's 9th Symphony. A new iPhone user assumed silent mode would silence everything, not realizing alarms bypass the mute switch. While acknowledging the tradeoff is tricky, Gruber argues the current behavior is correct and rejects suggestions to add confirmation dialogs. He contends that designing for the edge case of an accidental alarm at a symphony would cause far more harm — thousands oversleeping daily — than the rare public embarrassment.
key_points: The iPhone mute switch intentionally allows alarms to sound even in silent mode, which caused an embarrassing interruption at the New York Philharmonic
Adding alerts or confirmation prompts when toggling mute would not solve the problem, especially since users often flip the switch without looking at the phone
The current design correctly optimizes for the common case (not oversleeping) over the rare edge case (alarm going off in public)
You can't design around every single edge case, and attempting to do so often makes the product worse for the majority of users
money_quotes: Adding alerts and confirmation prompts is seldom a good idea, and would not help at all when you toggle the mute switch without even looking at the iPhone.
I think the current behavior of the iPhone mute switch is correct.

You can't design around every single edge case, and a new iPhone user who makes the reasonable but mistaken assumption that the mute switch silences everything, with an alarm set that he wasn't aware of, and who is sitting in the front row of the New York Philharmonic when the accidental alarm goes off, is a pretty good example of an edge case.
Whereas if the mute switch silenced everything, there'd be thousands of people oversleeping every single day because they went to bed the night before unaware that the phone was still in silent mode.
I frequently toggle that switch without taking the phone out of my pocket.
key_insight: Good design optimizes for the common case over the edge case, and the iPhone mute switch correctly prioritizes preventing thousands from oversleeping over preventing rare public embarrassments.
